title: Scalus: Cardano’s Application Platform for Building, Launching, and Scaling

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Scalus: Cardano’s Application Platform for Building, Launching, and Scaling

Deliverables: Protocol upgrades, critical integrations, DeFi liquidity, and VC capital are aligning., But the next bottleneck is no longer core infrastructure, it is application delivery., Teams still struggle to ship non-trivial applications because they must master and assemble too many moving parts themselves: smart contracts, formal verification, chain access, operations, and scaling., It lacks an integrated application platform that has it all and just works.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, low materiality): Protocol upgrades, critical integrations, DeFi liquidity, and VC capital are aligning.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, low materiality): But the next bottleneck is no longer core infrastructure, it is application delivery.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, low materiality): Teams still struggle to ship non-trivial applications because they must master and assemble too many moving parts themselves: smart contracts, formal verification, chain access, operations, and scaling.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, low materiality): It lacks an integrated application platform that has it all and just works.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, low materiality): The Application Layer Decides the Next Chapter Cardano has shipped the protocol foundations.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, low materiality): The question is no longer "can Cardano do X?" but "how fast can builders ship X?".
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Teams still struggle to ship non-trivial applications because they must master and assemble too many moving parts themselves: smart contracts, formal verification, chain access, operations, and scaling." and clears the evidence gates.
Strongest NO: an unresolved blocker (missing complete proposal summary) means costs or weak controls may outweigh the claimed benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
